Clone the TensorFlow Models repository from https://github.com/tensorflow/models.git at the latest release tag
Navigate to the 'research/delf' directory within the cloned repository
Install necessary Python packages including `tensorflow` version 2.x and `absl-py` using pip
Ensure that the installed TensorFlow version is compatible with the code in the repository
Create a new Python script named `reproduce_bug.py`
Import the required modules: `tf`, `parameterized` from `absl.testing`, and `delf_model` from `delf.python.training.model`
Define a batch size of 2 and image dimensions of 321x321 in the script
Create random uniform input data with shape (batch_size, height, width, 3) using TensorFlow's `tf.random.uniform` function
Call the `Delf` class from `delf.python.training.model.delf_model` with parameters `block3_strides=True` and `name='DELF'`
Initialize classifiers for the model by calling the `init_classifiers` method with `num_classes=1000`
Generate random uniform labels for training with shape (batch_size,) using TensorFlow's `tf.random.uniform` function
Define a loss object using `tf.keras.losses.SparseCategoricalCrossentropy(from_logits=True, reduction=tf.keras.losses.Reduction.NONE)`
Create a gradient tape context manager to record operations for automatic differentiation
Forward propagate the input images through the model and calculate descriptor prelogits and attention prelogits
Calculate descriptor logits and compute loss using the defined loss object
Calculate attention logits and compute loss using the defined loss object
Sum the descriptor and attention losses to get the total loss
Compute gradients of the total loss with respect to trainable weights using `gradient_tape.gradient` method
Clip gradients by global norm to prevent exploding gradients
Apply the clipped gradients to the model's trainable weights using the optimizer
Run the script in a Google Colab environment with a T4 GPU
Observe if an `AttributeError: module 'keras._tf_keras.keras.layers' has no attribute 'experimental'` occurs during execution
Verify that the error is reproducible by running the script multiple times
